source.title: The Ancient Faith Prayer Book
source.sourceUse: source-critical inventory and source witness; no prayer text imported in this tranche

scope: Eastern Orthodox Book of Needs / devotional-prayer source selection. This inventory identifies candidate prayers from the Ancient Faith Prayer Book structure while rejecting blanket ingestion of the book and rejecting assumptions that modern-context prayers are unique merely because they address modern needs.
governingDecisions: Do not ingest all prayers from this source.
Ancient Faith Prayer Book may be used as a source witness for widely received Orthodox prayers.
Do not treat Ancient Faith's compilation as a blank-check source for unique, modern, or publisher-created prayers.
If a prayer is ancient, biblical, liturgical, patristic, or commonly used across Orthodox prayer books, it may be selected for Universal Office intake after ordinary source review.
If the wording appears to be a distinctive modern translation, editorial adaptation, or publisher-created prayer, do not treat that wording as default runtime text without further review.
Do not assume a prayer is uncommon or unique merely because its need-category is modern. Determine independent Orthodox reception before excluding it.
Do not change runtime UI or tradition counts in this tranche.
notFirstPass: publisher-unique compiled devotional prayers without independent Orthodox reception
distinctive modern translations or adaptations until wording review is complete
marriage/fertility/pregnancy/miscarriage prayers unless independently attested and pastorally reviewed

candidates.title: The Trisagion Prayers
candidates.sourceSection: Morning Prayers; Afternoon Prayers; Late Evening Prayers; Thanksgiving After Holy Communion
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Use an independent received Orthodox prayer-book or liturgical source witness before adding runtime text.
candidates.reason: The Trisagion Prayers are broadly received in Orthodox daily prayer and have been implemented using common received Orthodox wording while treating the Ancient Faith Prayer Book as a source witness.

candidates.title: The Jesus Prayer
candidates.sourceSection: Prayers for Various Needs and Occasions / Spiritual Life
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Use independently received wording rather than treating this compilation as the controlling source.
candidates.reason: The Jesus Prayer is a central and widely received Orthodox devotional prayer, not unique to this source; it has been implemented using the common received short form while treating the Ancient Faith Prayer Book as a source witness.

candidates.title: The Prayer of Manasseh
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Prefer the app's Bible/source corpus or another public-domain scripture witness for runtime text.
candidates.reason: This is a scriptural penitential prayer and a strong repentance candidate, but the runtime text should come from a scripture source witness rather than the compilation.

candidates.title: Psalm 50
candidates.sourceSection: Morning Prayers; Late Evening Prayers; Prayers for Confession
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Use the app's psalter/scripture corpus rather than the compilation text.
candidates.reason: Psalm 50 is the standard Orthodox penitential psalm and belongs naturally in repentance contexts.

candidates.title: Prayer of St. Mardarios
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Implemented using common received Orthodox wording with Ancient Faith Prayer Book as a source witness.
candidates.reason: A common Orthodox evening prayer with ancient attribution; implemented using common received wording while treating the Ancient Faith Prayer Book as a source witness.

candidates.title: Prayer of St. Philaret of Moscow
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Implemented using common received Orthodox wording with Ancient Faith Prayer Book as a source witness.
candidates.reason: A widely used Orthodox morning prayer; implemented using common received wording while treating the Ancient Faith Prayer Book as a source witness.

candidates.title: Prayers Before Holy Communion
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Treat as communion-preparation material, not ordinary Book of Needs default content.
candidates.reason: Common and important Orthodox devotional material, but belongs in a communion-preparation lane rather than the first Book of Needs needs-prayer batch.

candidates.title: Thanksgiving After Holy Communion
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Treat as communion-thanksgiving material, not ordinary Book of Needs default content.
candidates.reason: Common Orthodox prayer material, but feature placement should be communion-specific.

candidates.title: Before Reading Scripture
candidates.sourceSection: Prayers for Various Needs and Occasions / Spiritual Life
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Implemented using common received Orthodox wording with Ancient Faith Prayer Book as a source witness.
candidates.reason: A common Orthodox prayer before reading the Gospel or Scripture; implemented as lay-devotional preparation for Scripture reading.

candidates.title: Before a Journey
candidates.sourceSection: Prayers for Various Needs and Occasions / Work, Study, and Recreation
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Prefer Big Book of Needs or another Orthodox service/prayer-book source for journey prayers.
candidates.reason: The need category is appropriate, but the text should be corroborated outside this modern compilation.

candidates.title: Prayer of a Sick Person
candidates.sourceSection: Prayers for Various Needs and Occasions / Sickness and Health
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Prefer Big Book of Needs or independently received Orthodox sources for sickness prayers.
candidates.reason: The need category is central to Book of Needs, but this compilation should not be the sole source for runtime wording.

candidates.title: Prayer for a Sick Person
candidates.sourceSection: Prayers for Various Needs and Occasions / Sickness and Health
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Prefer Big Book of Needs or independently received Orthodox sources for sickness prayers.
candidates.reason: Potentially useful lay-devotional content, but requires independent source comparison.

candidates.title: Before Using the Internet
candidates.sourceSection: Prayers for Various Needs and Occasions / Work, Study, and Recreation
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Research independent Orthodox reception before excluding or using this prayer. Do not assume uniqueness merely because the need-category is modern.
candidates.reason: Modern-context prayer; research is required to determine whether it has independent Orthodox reception, parish/prayer-book circulation, clergy use, or repeated appearance across Orthodox sources.

candidates.title: Marriage, family, pregnancy, miscarriage, and infant-death prayers
candidates.sourceSection: Prayers for Various Needs and Occasions / Marriage and Family
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Handle through dedicated pastoral review and comparison with Big Book of Needs/source witnesses.
candidates.reason: Pastorally important but sensitive; not appropriate for casual bulk ingestion from a modern compilation.

candidates.title: Prayers of the Saints
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Use individual saint/source witnesses rather than this anthology as controlling source.
candidates.reason: The section mixes ancient, Western, Orthodox, and modern saint material; selection must be source-specific.

candidates.title: Prayer of St. Ephrem the Syrian
candidates.sourceSection: Morning Prayers; Afternoon Prayers; Evening Prayers; Late Evening Prayers
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Implemented using common received Orthodox wording with Ancient Faith Prayer Book as a source witness.
candidates.reason: A widely received Orthodox Lenten prayer; implemented using common received wording while treating the Ancient Faith Prayer Book as a source witness.

candidates.title: A Prayer for Every Hour
candidates.sourceSection: Afternoon Prayers; Early Evening Prayers; Late Evening Prayers
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Implemented using common received Orthodox wording with Ancient Faith Prayer Book as a source witness.
candidates.reason: A broadly received Orthodox prayer used in the Hours; implemented as lay-devotional daily-use material.

candidates.title: Before Work
candidates.sourceSection: Prayers for Various Needs and Occasions / Work, Study, and Recreation
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Implemented using common received Orthodox wording with Ancient Faith Prayer Book as a source witness.
candidates.reason: A common Orthodox prayer before beginning work; implemented as lay-devotional daily-use material.

candidates.title: Before Study

candidates.title: Before Meals
candidates.preferredSourcePath: Implemented using common received Orthodox wording with Ancient Faith Prayer Book as a source witness.
candidates.reason: A common Orthodox blessing before meals; implemented as lay-devotional daily-use material.

researchPrinciples.prayerUnitVsExactWording: The prayer unit and the exact English wording are distinct review questions. A common Orthodox prayer may be appropriate for intake even when its exact wording needs comparison across witnesses.
researchPrinciples.ancientFaithUse: Ancient Faith may serve as a source witness, comparison witness, and in some cases a runtime-text witness after review. It is not a blanket authorization for bulk ingestion.
researchPrinciples.modernContextRule: Modern-context need categories require research. They are not automatically excluded and not automatically accepted.

needsResearchBeforeDisposition: prayers whose need-category is plausible but whose independent Orthodox reception has not yet been checked
